players.name: Diogo Costa
players.bio: Porto's shot-stopper is one of Europe's most composed young keepers. Known for his penalty heroics — he saved three in a single shootout at Euro 2024 — Diogo Costa is Portugal's wall between the sticks.
players.facts: Saved 3 penalties in one shootout vs Slovenia at Euro 2024. Yes, three. In a row. Calm as you like.

players.name: Nélson Semedo
players.bio: A tireless right-back who has served Portugal with distinction across major tournaments. Now plying his trade in Istanbul with Fenerbahçe, Semedo brings experience, pace, and defensive reliability to the backline.
players.facts: Born in Lisbon to Cape Verdean parents, Semedo once played futsal before switching to the beautiful game full-time.

players.name: Rúben Dias
players.bio: Manchester City's colossus at the back, Rúben Dias is a commanding centre-back who won the Premier League Player of the Season in 2020-21. Cool under pressure and ferocious in the tackle, he anchors Portugal's defence.
players.facts: Won the PFA Players' Player of the Year in 2021 — the first Portuguese player ever to claim that honour.

players.name: Tomás Araújo
players.bio: A composed and technically gifted centre-back coming through Benfica's famed academy. Tomás Araújo is one of Portugal's most exciting defensive prospects, earning his senior caps with maturity beyond his 24 years.
players.facts: Born in 2002, Araújo is among the youngest defenders in this Portugal squad — but reads the game like a veteran.

players.name: Diogo Dalot
players.bio: Born in Braga, Dalot is a modern full-back equally at home going forward or defending. A product of Porto's academy who matured at Manchester United, he's become a key figure for Portugal's right flank.
players.facts: Diogo Dalot was signed by José Mourinho for Man United in 2018 — a fellow Portuguese keeping it in the family.

players.name: Matheus Nunes
players.bio: Born in Brazil, raised in Portugal, Matheus Nunes is a dynamic box-to-box midfielder who joined Manchester City for €60m. His energy and technical quality make him a vital cog in Portugal's midfield engine room.
players.facts: Nunes was born in Rio de Janeiro but chose Portugal over Brazil — a decision Tite probably still thinks about.

players.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
players.bio: The GOAT debate will rage forever, but CR7's numbers are simply alien. The all-time top international scorer with 143 goals in 228 caps, Ronaldo arrives at his sixth World Cup at 41, still hungry, still iconic.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Superfly VII Elite FG
players.facts: Ronaldo has scored at 5 consecutive World Cups — no European player has ever matched that feat. Six is the mission.

players.name: Bruno Fernandes
players.bio: Captain, talisman, and Portugal's creative heartbeat. Bruno Fernandes orchestrates play with vision and intensity, and his knack for big-game goals makes him indispensable. Love him or loathe him — you can't ignore him.
players.facts: Bruno scores or assists roughly every 73 minutes for Portugal. He also perfected the art of the dramatic free-kick celebration.

players.name: Gonçalo Ramos
players.bio: From Olhão to the world stage — Gonçalo Ramos announced himself with a hat-trick against Switzerland at the 2022 World Cup aged just 21. PSG's clinical striker is dangerous, direct, and deadly in the box.
players.facts: Ramos became only the 4th player ever to score a WC hat-trick as a substitute — on his World Cup debut, no less.

players.name: Bernardo Silva
players.full_name: Bernardo Mota Veiga de Carvalho e Silva
players.bio: Arguably Portugal's most technically gifted player, Bernardo Silva is a serial winner with Man City and a relentless workhorse in attack. Small in stature, enormous in impact — the complete modern footballer.
players.facts: Bernardo has won 6 Premier League titles with Man City. He also once dribbled past 7 players in training and apologised afterwards.

players.name: João Félix
players.bio: João Félix burst onto the scene as a €126m teenager at Atlético Madrid and has spent his career living up — and occasionally down — to that billing. Gifted, creative, and now reunited with Ronaldo at Al-Nassr.
players.facts: Félix became the third most expensive transfer in history at age 19. The pressure? He's used to it by now.

players.name: José Sá
players.bio: Born in Kumasi, Ghana, José Sá is a goalkeeper — not a forward as his squad position suggests. A capable backup stopper who served Wolves with distinction, he provides experienced cover for Diogo Costa at this tournament.
players.facts: José Sá was born in Ghana but qualifies for Portugal — making him one of the more globe-spanning stories in the squad.

players.name: Renato Veiga
players.bio: A versatile and athletic defender who can play centre-back or as a defensive midfielder. Renato Veiga made his mark at Chelsea before moving to Villarreal and is one of Portugal's brightest young defensive talents.
players.facts: Veiga is so versatile he's been deployed in midfield for club and country — defenders who can do that are rare gold.

players.name: Gonçalo Inácio
players.bio: A left-footed centre-back with composure and quality in possession, Gonçalo Inácio has been a cornerstone of Sporting CP's defence. One of the most promising young defenders in Iberian football right now.
players.facts: Inácio scored a memorable goal for Sporting in the Champions League — not bad for someone whose job is stopping them.

players.name: João Neves
players.bio: Son of former Portugal international Luís Neves, João Neves is a prodigious defensive midfielder who joined PSG from Benfica. At just 21, he controls games with a calmness that belies his age entirely.
players.facts: João Neves' father Luís also played for Portugal — making them one of international football's most quietly impressive dynasties.

players.name: Francisco Trincão
players.full_name: Francisco António Machado Mota de Castro Trincão
players.bio: A winger with flair, vision and an eye for goal, Francisco Trincão has flourished at Sporting CP after loan spells shaped his development. His creativity and direct running give Portugal a sharp attacking option.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Vapor XIII Elite FG
players.facts: Trincão joined Barcelona for €31m aged 20 but found true form only after leaving — a tale as old as time at Camp Nou.

players.name: Rafael Leão
players.bio: Rafael Leão is electrifying — a pacy, skillful left winger who can turn a game on its head in seconds. A key figure at AC Milan and one of Europe's most sought-after attackers, he lights up every stage he plays on.
players.facts: Leão has been clocked at over 36km/h during a Serie A match — making him one of football's fastest players on the ball.

players.name: Pedro Neto
players.bio: A tricky, rapid winger who loves to run at defenders, Pedro Neto has been a fan favourite at Chelsea. His career has been interrupted by serious injuries, making every appearance feel like a small triumph of resilience.
players.facts: Neto suffered two major knee injuries in his early career — each time he came back quicker and more direct than before.

players.name: Gonçalo Guedes
players.bio: A skilful and versatile attacker who scored the goal that won Portugal the 2022 UEFA Nations League. Gonçalo Guedes offers pace, creativity and big-game experience — a proven performer at the highest level.
players.facts: Guedes scored the only goal in the 2019 Nations League final vs Netherlands — Portugal's first ever Nations League title.

players.name: João Cancelo
players.bio: One of the world's most attack-minded full-backs, João Cancelo is a marauding, elegant presence on either flank. After a turbulent exit from Man City, he's revived his career brilliantly at Barcelona.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Vapor XIII Elite FG
players.facts: Cancelo has played for Juventus, Inter, Man City, Bayern (on loan) and Barcelona — a CV that reads like a wishlist.

players.name: Rúben Neves
players.bio: A technically superb defensive midfielder who captained Porto in the Champions League at just 20. Rúben Neves is now in the Saudi Pro League with Al-Hilal but remains a composed, authoritative presence for Portugal.
players.facts: Rúben Neves became Porto's youngest ever captain in the Champions League at age 20 — a record that still stands.

players.name: Rui Silva
players.bio: A reliable and experienced goalkeeper, Rui Silva has been a consistent performer in La Liga before joining Sporting CP. With just 3 caps, this World Cup could be his biggest stage — if called upon.
players.facts: Rui Silva was born in Maia, the same town as Bruno Fernandes. Great place for producing Portugal internationals, apparently.

players.name: Vitinha
players.bio: Vitinha is Porto's academy jewel turned PSG metronome. A technically immaculate central midfielder who controls tempo with quiet authority, he's the engine Portugal's midfield barely knew it needed.
players.facts: Vitinha was released by Man City's academy as a teenager. PSG paid €40m for him shortly after. City fans, look away.

players.name: Samú Costa
players.bio: A hardworking and intelligent defensive midfielder, Samú Costa has impressed at Mallorca with his tenacity and positioning. With just 6 caps, he's one of the less-heralded names in this squad — but no less important.
players.facts: Samú Costa plays for Mallorca — making him one of very few Portugal internationals based outside Europe's top five leagues.

players.name: Nuno Mendes
players.bio: A flying left-back with tremendous athleticism and an eye for a cross, Nuno Mendes has established himself as one of Europe's finest in his position at PSG. At just 23, he has the world at his feet — literally.
players.facts: Nuno Mendes made his PSG debut aged 19 and was immediately compared to Marcelo. High praise — and not entirely wrong.

players.name: Francisco Conceição
players.bio: Son of Porto legend Sérgio Conceição, Francisco is carving his own path with flair and fearlessness. A skilful right midfielder now at Juventus, he scored a famous late winner for Portugal — fathers, take note.
players.facts: Francisco's father Sérgio managed Porto while Francisco played there — family meetings must have been complicated affairs.
